page: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 160 virtual CPUs mapped to 80 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. It achieves top 10% placement in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 14657.53 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 464.03 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The platform also excels in database and key-value operations, recording 200.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a top-tier score in Passmark database operations.

A key architectural tradeoff of the D160as_v7 is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. Additionally, the instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server provides high resource density and top-tier compute performance relative to its hardware class, making it a viable option for large-scale deployments that do not require local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration.

This configuration is highly suited for demanding general-purpose enterprise workloads. It is optimized for large-scale web serving, high-throughput database operations, memory-intensive application servers, and CPU-bound tasks such as data compression or large-scale software compilation. It also supports CPU-based large language model inference, demonstrating top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for medium and large models.
description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, delivering 160 vCPUs across 80 physical cores, paired with 640.0 GB of system memory. Benchmark data places this server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside exceptional memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities, such as 200.12 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, requiring external Premium Storage, and the absence of a dedicated GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This instance is highly suited for enterprise-grade web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 160 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 640 GB RAM, delivering top-tier compute and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 640 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose virtual machine delivering 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 640 GB RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 160 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs and 640.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and static web serving
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Well-suited for CPU-based large language model inference
